title: The NCC Team: Ice, Fire, and Everyone Around You

objectives.outcome: Ice and Fire teams: coverage, patient lists, downgrade pathways
Every team role and what each is responsible for
Correct TigerConnect role for time of day and question type

didactic_sections.heading: Attending Coverage
key_points: Rounds 8 AM (or after grand rounds Friday)
Night neurointensivist in-house until ~midnight most weeks

didactic_sections.heading: When to Call the Attending
triggers: Hypotension on pressors, arrhythmia, troponin, transfusion
Worsening respiratory status, intubation watch, NIV start, peak/plateau changes
Anytime you feel overwhelmed or unsure

didactic_sections.heading: Residents
key_points: 1 neurosurgery resident per Ice team typically
Each resident sees up to 8 patients
Clinic + Fridays + weekends off-limits as off-days

didactic_sections.heading: Pharmacy / RT / Nursing / EEG / SW / Therapy

responsibilities: HTS, sedation, abx, AC reversal, pentobarbital, PRIS monitoring
Read and report via Ice/Fire+EEG TigerConnect

didactic_sections.heading: Note Signature Discipline
key_points: Include ASCOM #, daytime end time, nighttime contact

nursing_considerations: Charge RN is the operational quarterback — loop them in early for bed/cohorting questions
Bedside RN owns the whiteboard accuracy; verify your name and ASCOM are correct
pharm_considerations: NCC pharmacist is on rounds — bring sedation, HTS, AC reversal, and abx questions to that interaction rather than paging mid-day
Pharmacist owns pentobarbital coma protocol monitoring (q72h LFTs, propylene glycol watch)

visuals.subject: Ice and Fire team tree with roles
visuals.notes: Includes APP, residents (neuro and NSGY), fellows, pharmacy, RT, charge, RN, EEG, SW, therapy

visuals.subject: 24-hour attending coverage timeline
visuals.notes: Shows daytime, evening, in-house-until-midnight, phone-after, PCCM 24/7

visuals.subject: TigerConnect role cheat sheet
visuals.notes: Printable wallet card and in-app quick view

visuals.subject: When to call the attending — non-negotiable triggers
visuals.notes: Used in M01.L3 and revisited in M03.L6

assessment.item: Identify TigerConnect role for new admission at 2 AM
Fire team downgrade goes to which general neurology team?
RN calls re: vent dyssynchrony at 11 PM — who do you notify in addition to NCC?
